citation: Bianchi, D.M., Pelletier, J.F., Hutchison, C.A. III, Glass, J.I., Luthey-Schulten, Z., et al. Toward the Complete Functional Characterization of a Minimal Bacterial Proteome. Journal of Physical Chemistry B (2022).
notes: Per-gene proteomic reference used by Thornburg 2022 + 2026 as the initial-protein-copy-number source. Our staged initial_concentrations.xlsx 'Comparative Proteomics' sheet (455 rows of Locus_Tag / Gene_Name / Gene_Product / Protein_Length / Exp.Ptn.Cnt / Essentiality / Primary_Function / ...) is the distilled form of this paper's measurements. The 'Essentiality' column that powers our Layer 6 ground truth (syn3a_essentiality_breuer2019) originally comes from Hutchison 2016 transposon data, but the measured per-gene copy-number column comes from this paper.
caveats: We have not separately verified that our staged 'Exp.Ptn.Cnt' column matches Bianchi 2022's published table. If future Layer 1/3 work needs per-gene copy-number validation with error bars, the original paper's supplementary table is the better source than the Luthey-Schulten distillation.
